title: Platform Security Policy & Procedure
description: Covers PR.PS-01 through PR.PS-06 (6 controls). Configuration management, software maintenance/patching, hardware maintenance, log generation and management, unauthorized software controls, secure development lifecycle. Existing: scheduled-maintenance.md, faq.md (no Docker policy).

title: Network Architecture Diagram (ID.AM-03)
description: CRITICAL: Tier 2 gate requirement. Create diagram showing login nodes, compute nodes, storage, management network, external connectivity, firewall boundaries, and data flow directions. Covers ID.AM-03.

title: Data Protection Policy & Procedure
description: Covers PR.DS-01.01, PR.DS-02.01, PR.DS-10.01, PR.DS-11.01 (4 controls). Data classification, encryption (at rest, in transit, in use), backup procedures, backup testing, retention/disposal. Existing: general.md, shared-storage.md cover data management and backup status.

title: Security Awareness & Training Policy & Procedure
description: Covers PR.AT-01.01, PR.AT-02.01 (2 controls; both have existing Google Doc policies). Training program, general cybersecurity awareness, specialized role training for techstaff, training records, periodic refresher schedule.

title: Infrastructure Resilience Policy & Procedure
description: Covers PR.IR-01 through PR.IR-04 (4 controls). Network access controls, environmental protections (power, cooling, fire suppression), resilience mechanisms, capacity planning/monitoring.

title: Incident Response Plan
description: Covers RS.MA-01 through RS.MA-05, RS.AN-03/06/07/08, RS.CO-02/03, RS.MI-01/02 (13 controls). Full IRP: declaration criteria, triage, categorization, escalation, containment, eradication, evidence preservation, root cause analysis, stakeholder communication, recovery initiation. HPC scenarios: cryptomining, compromised credentials, GPU driver vulns. Existing: techstaff-sla.md, RS.MI-01/02 Google Doc policies.

title: Risk Assessment Policy & Procedure
description: Covers ID.RA-01, ID.RA-02, ID.RA-03, ID.RA-04, ID.RA-05, ID.RA-06, ID.RA-07, ID.RA-08, ID.RA-10 (9 controls; 5 have existing Google Doc policies). Vulnerability management, threat intelligence, risk analysis, risk response tracking, change management, vulnerability disclosure, supplier evaluation.

title: Continuous Improvement Policy & Procedure
description: Covers ID.IM-01 through ID.IM-04 (4 controls). Improvement identification from evaluations, security tests/exercises, operational processes, and incident response plan maintenance.

title: Access Management Policy & Procedure
description: Covers PR.AA-01.01 through PR.AA-06.01 (6 controls). End-to-end access lifecycle: identity proofing, provisioning, authentication, authorization, periodic review, deprovisioning, physical access. Existing: accounts.md, ssh.md, general.md. Gaps: formal identity proofing, access review, key rotation, physical access docs.

title: Monitoring & Detection Policy & Procedure
description: Covers DE.CM-01/02/03/06/09, DE.AE-02/03/04/06/07/08 (11 controls; 4 have existing Google Doc policies). Network monitoring, physical environment monitoring, personnel activity monitoring, external service monitoring, compute environment monitoring, event analysis, information correlation, impact assessment, event notification, threat intelligence integration, incident declaration criteria.

title: Asset Management Policy & Procedure
description: Covers ID.AM-01.01, ID.AM-02.01, ID.AM-08.01 (3 controls). Hardware inventory, software/services inventory, lifecycle management. Existing: cluster-information.md, purchasing.md, ID.AM-01.01 Google Doc policy. Gaps: formal software inventory, lifecycle procedure.

title: Recovery Plan & Procedure
description: Covers RC.RP-01 through RC.RP-06, RC.CO-03/04 (8 controls; RC.CO-03 has existing Google Doc policy). Recovery execution, action prioritization, backup integrity verification, post-incident norms, asset restoration validation, recovery closure criteria, recovery communication. HPC-specific: node rebuild, SLURM DB backup/restore, user data restoration, RTO/RPO.
